name: Broken streetlights near St Thomas' Hospital
description: Three streetlights in a row are out on Lambeth Palace Road, between the roundabout and the entrance to St Thomas' Hospital. The whole stretch is completely dark after 9pm. There have been muggings in this area recently and it feels very unsafe for staff leaving the hospital on the night shift. I reported this two weeks ago and nothing has been done.
photo_description: Dark residential stretch of road at night. Three consecutive lamp posts visible, all unlit. Contrast with illuminated section further down the road and lit hospital building in background. Pavement is barely visible. A pedestrian silhouette walking on the dark stretch. Road surface wet with no reflections from overhead lighting. Hospital entrance sign partially visible at far end of the dark zone.

location.address: Lambeth Palace Road, between roundabout and St Thomas' Hospital entrance

metadata.source: Based on real FixMyStreet street lighting reports from Lambeth and Westminster. Coordinates place the report on Lambeth Palace Road, approximately 150m from St Thomas' Hospital main entrance.
severity_factors: Near St Thomas' Hospital (~150m) — staff safety at night
Multiple lights out (3) creating extended dark zone
Previously reported 2 weeks ago — no action taken
escalation_path: Day 0: Report filed to Lambeth Council Highways
Day 7: No acknowledgement — automated follow-up email
Day 14: No action — phone escalation via ElevenLabs + Twilio
Day 21: If unresolved — formal complaint to Local Government Ombudsman
Day 30: If unresolved — draft letter to local MP
